commit.message:
fix(console): organization column display in grants #10822 (#11742)

# Which Problems Are Solved

- The organization name in the user grants table was not consistently
displayed. The `grantedOrgName` property was sometimes missing,
resulting in an empty or incorrect value in the 'org' column.

# How the Problems Are Solved

- Implements a conditional check for the 'org' column in the user grants
table. The cell now displays `grantedOrgName` if available, otherwise it
falls back to `orgName`.

# Additional Changes

- Imports and adds `TypeSafeCellDefModule` to the user grants module,
providing the `cnslCellDef` directive for more flexible and type-safe
cell definitions.

# Additional Context

- Fixes #10822

commit.message:
fix: sunburst center click, deeper tree fetch, and LRU cache

Three fixes:

1. Center click broken: SVG <text> elements overlaid the <circle> and
   swallowed click events. Added pointer-events:none to both text
   elements so clicks pass through to the circle. Also added a small
   '▲ UP' indicator when drilled down.

2. Shallow data: increased FETCH_DEPTH from 5 to 8 so nested folders
   are covered. Now passes showHidden prop through from workspace-
   explorer so hidden files are included when the toggle is on.

3. No caching: every folder selection re-fetched the deep tree. Added a
   16-entry LRU cache keyed by path+showHidden, invalidated on
   workspace-update events.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(schema-org): allow null to opt out of default values (#680)

* fix(schema-org): allow null to opt out of default values

When a user sets a schema-org property to null, it is now removed from
the final output instead of being overwritten by setIfEmpty defaults or
inheritMeta. null acts as an explicit opt-out sentinel that is preserved
through the full resolution pipeline and stripped only after all
resolvers (including resolveRootNode) have run.

Closes #548

* fix(schema-org): allow null to opt out of default values (#687)

commit.message:
fix(cdn): orphan cleaner not cleaning step-log and service-log items (#7657)

Two issues prevented the orphan cleaner from cleaning up CDN items
whose workflow run no longer exists:

1. Stuck queue: the query ORDER BY created ASC LIMIT N always
   re-selected the same oldest non-orphan items, blocking newer
   orphaned items. Fixed by using a cursor-based pagination on the
   created timestamp, advancing through the full item set across
   successive batches.

2. Project-unaware check: ExistRunByID only checked the workflow_run
   table without verifying the parent project still exists. Orphaned
   workflow_run rows (left after project deletion, no CASCADE) were
   reported as existing. Fixed by adding a JOIN on the project table.

Signed-off-by: francois.samin <francois.samin@ovhcloud.com>

commit.message:
fix: model switching — return structured metadata and broadcast model_changed

Three issues prevented model switching from working:

1. SlashCommandResult lacked model_label/thinking_level/supports_thinking
   fields, so the backend could never pass state back to the frontend.

2. The agents.py command response only returned {status, message} — the
   frontend expected model_label, thinking_level, supports_thinking in
   the command object but they were always undefined.

3. No model_changed SSE event was broadcast after /model or /thinking
   commands, so other connected clients never saw the change. The
   frontend handler at app.js:1540 was dead code.

Fixes:
- Add optional metadata fields to SlashCommandResult dataclass
- _handle_model now queries Pi state after set_model to get thinking
  support info and populates all metadata fields
- _handle_thinking populates model_label and thinking_level on success
- agents.py builds the command response with all metadata fields and
  broadcasts model_changed SSE event to all clients

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Use Popover API for code snippet tooltips; fix scroll-offset positioning (#1061)

* Use Popover API for tooltip divs; fix scroll-offset positioning bug

- Add `popover` attribute to `div.fsdocs-tip` elements in HtmlFormatting.fs,
  placing them in the browser top layer when supported (Baseline 2024).
- Update fsdocs-tips.js to call showPopover()/hidePopover() on browsers that
  support the Popover API; fall back to display:block/none on older browsers.
- Switch to `position: fixed` in the Popover API path (correct for top-layer
  elements) and fix a scroll-offset bug where tooltips appeared at wrong
  positions when the page was scrolled.
- Fix a minor bug in the right-edge overflow correction (was using `y` instead
  of `x` as the base for the left-shift calculation).
- Add `div.fsdocs-tip:popover-open { display: block }` to fsdocs-default.css
  so author-level display:none does not suppress the Popover API reveal.
- Update RELEASE_NOTES.md.

Closes #422

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger CI checks

* fix: resolve popover black-background regression; drop fallback; add fade-in

- CSS: add `inset: unset` to `div.fsdocs-tip:popover-open` to reset the UA
  popover stylesheet's `inset: 0` (i.e. right:0 / bottom:0), which was
  stretching the element across the viewport and causing the visible dark area
  around the tooltip.
- CSS: add `position: fixed` explicitly in the :popover-open rule so the
  element is correctly anchored to the JS-supplied left/top coordinates.
- CSS: add a 120ms ease-out opacity fade-in animation for a subtle reveal.
- JS: remove the display-toggle fallback branch entirely. The Popover API is
  Baseline 2024 (Chrome 114+, Firefox 125+, Safari 17+) and the technical
  audience of fsdocs users will have modern browsers.
- RELEASE_NOTES: update entry to reflect the simplified (no-fallback) design
  and the animation addition.

which are undefined in the current design system — causing invisible text.

Replacements:

  var(--faint) → var(--mist)   (secondary/muted text)
  var(--muted) → var(--mist)   (same)

commit.message:
Fix: replace platform-specific strftime format with portable alternative

Replace %-d (Linux-only strftime format) with .day attribute in f-string
for cross-platform compatibility. The %-d format specifier is not part of
the C standard and fails on Windows and some non-POSIX platforms.

Uses start.day which naturally produces unpadded day numbers on all
platforms.
